Alaska · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Examiners in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology under the Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$250
$250 application and license fee
Renewal
$250
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Examiners in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 110806, Juneau, AK 99811-0806

Physical Address

333 Willoughby Ave., 9th Floor, Juneau, AK 99801

Phone & Fax

Phone: (907) 465-2580

Fax: (907) 465-2974

Email

audiology-slp@alaska.gov

Credential Categories

Alaska SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Alaska. Requires a master's degree, clinical fellowship, and passing Praxis score.

Provisional SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice in Alaska.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Alaska Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours) under an ASHA-certified SLP.

Submit Application to the Board

Submit the application with the $250 fee. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, CF verification, and background check.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.
  • Background checkFingerprint-based criminal background check.
Telepractice

Alaska Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Alaska permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines. Telepractice is particularly important in Alaska given the state's rural and remote communities.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Alaska issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship, allowing supervised practice while fulfilling post-graduate experience requirements.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Alaska is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alaska through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available.

Frequently Asked Questions

Alaska SLP FAQ

How much does an Alaska SLP license cost?

The application and license fee is $250. Biennial renewal is also $250. Alaska has higher licensing fees compared to most other states.

Is Alaska part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Alaska has enacted ASLP-IC legislation, allowing compact privileges for SLPs from other member states.

Does Alaska allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Alaska permits telepractice and it is widely used due to the state's remote geography.
